candidates.flash_summary: Full CFO ownership of €35M Belgian MNC subsidiary with 22 years across EPC heavyweights (Andritz, ABB, Tata Projects, Danieli). Matrix reports to Global CFO Belgium — strongest technical and governance match.
key_strengths: Full CFO ownership of €35M Belgian MNC subsidiary with matrix reporting to Global CFO Belgium
22 years across major EPC companies (Danieli, ABB, Tata Projects, Andritz) — deepest construction finance experience in shortlist
Genuine JV governance exposure managing Tata Projects–Alse joint venture for Dedicated Freight Corridor

edc_data.current_company: Sarens Heavy Lift India Pvt Ltd

scope_match.candidate_actual: Heavy crane EPC, steel plant EPC, hydropower
Reports to Belgium HQ; SE Asia projects

key_criteria.name: End-to-End Finance Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Full CFO ownership of €35M entity — budgeting, statutory reporting, treasury, working capital, transfer pricing, IFRS and US GAAP reporting, ECB financing, and cross-border taxation.

key_criteria.name: Construction / EPC Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 22 years across major EPC companies — deep percentage-of-completion accounting, consortium execution, contract negotiation, and claims management across Danieli, Alstom, Tata Projects, ABB, and Andritz.

key_criteria.name: JV Stakeholder Management
key_criteria.evidence: Managed Tata Projects–Alse JV for Dedicated Freight Corridor railway infrastructure — genuine corporate JV governance experience, not just bid-stage arrangements.

key_criteria.name: Board Governance & Legal Entity Responsibility
key_criteria.evidence: Monthly presentations to global CEO/CFO — supports demergers and due diligence, manages multinational regulatory compliance. Board interaction is monthly, not quarterly.

key_criteria.name: International / Multinational Exposure
key_criteria.evidence: Matrix reports to Global CFO in Belgium — manages cross-border subsidiary operations across India, Bangladesh, and SE Asian project sites (Vietnam, Laos, Indonesia, Myanmar, Nepal).

compensation.flexibility: No LTI, car, or additional perks currently. Motivated by broader autonomous JV mandate — compensation is not the primary driver.

why_interested.headline: Broader autonomous JV leadership mandate
why_interested.detail: Interested in stepping into a role with full autonomous finance leadership of a structured JV — a step up from subsidiary CFO to JV finance director.

why_interested.headline: European parent company aligns with career trajectory
why_interested.detail: Career has consistently moved toward larger MNCs with European HQs (Belgian, Italian, Swiss, German companies) — Dywidag fits the pattern.

why_interested.headline: Location is ideal
why_interested.detail: Lives 30 minutes from Okhla Phase 3, South Delhi — zero commute constraint.

potential_concerns.concern: No material red flags identified — strongest overall match against all five criteria.

our_take.text: Surajit is the strongest candidate in this shortlist by a clear margin. He is the only candidate with full multinational CFO-level EPC experience, genuine JV governance exposure, IFRS reporting, board interaction, and direct group reporting to a European HQ. His 22-year career has been a deliberate progression through increasingly senior finance roles at major EPC companies, and his current matrix reporting to Belgium mirrors the exact structure Dywidag requires. No material gaps identified.
discussion_points: Progress to final interviews with CEO India and Regional CFO

our_take_fragments: Strongest candidate in the shortlist — only one with full multinational CFO-level EPC experience and European HQ reporting
22-year career progression through Danieli, ABB, Tata Projects, Andritz, and Sarens — deliberate move toward larger MNC finance roles
Matrix reporting to Belgium mirrors the exact Dywidag structure — minimal adjustment period
Genuine JV governance from Tata Projects–Alse Dedicated Freight Corridor — not just bid-stage arrangements
Lives 30 minutes from Okhla — zero commute constraint

candidates.flash_summary: 15+ years in EPC water infrastructure finance with extensive JV creation and consortium banking (22 banks, €445M exposure). Currently treasury-heavy at UFLEX — motivated to return to EPC.
key_strengths: 15+ years in EPC water infrastructure finance with extensive JV creation and consortium banking
Manages consortium of 22 banks with €445M credit exposure — deep treasury and banking expertise
Grew Overseas Infrastructure Alliance from zero to €350–400M peak turnover over 13-year tenure

key_criteria.name: End-to-End Finance Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Strong treasury and corporate finance — manages consortium of 22 banks with €445M credit exposure, fund raising for CAPEX and working capital. Less recent full FP&A ownership.

key_criteria.name: Construction / EPC Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 15+ years in EPC water infrastructure at JWIL Infra and Overseas Infrastructure Alliance — project cash flows, milestone disbursements, banking instruments, and construction-specific accounting.

key_criteria.name: JV Stakeholder Management
key_criteria.evidence: Created multiple JVs in water sector with established companies — experience fulfilling financial and technical criteria for project awards across infrastructure ventures.

key_criteria.name: Board Governance & Legal Entity Responsibility
key_criteria.evidence: Finance committee member at Overseas Infrastructure Alliance — regular board presentations and strategic financial input over 13-year tenure. Grew company from zero to €350–400M peak turnover.

key_criteria.name: International / Multinational Exposure
key_criteria.evidence: International project finance across multiple African countries (Ethiopia, Mozambique, Tanzania, Congo, Rwanda) — global banking relationships and cross-border project structuring.

compensation.flexibility: No variable or LTI currently. Has own car and driver. One month notice period.

why_interested.headline: Wants to return to EPC from manufacturing
why_interested.detail: Joined UFLEX 'by mistake' after leaving construction — core expertise remains in banking, treasury, and corporate finance for EPC environments.

why_interested.headline: EPC-focused finance role with broader mandate
why_interested.detail: Interested in a role where his industry-specific experience in construction finance, JV structuring, and banking can be fully utilised.

potential_concerns.concern: Current role is more treasury-heavy than full-cycle finance — less recent statutory/FP&A ownership since moving to manufacturing sector.
English communication adequate but not exceptional — occasionally answers different questions than asked.

our_take.text: Tushar is a strong alternative with deep EPC infrastructure finance and banking expertise. His 15+ years in water infrastructure EPC gives him genuine construction finance depth, and his JV creation experience is directly relevant. The board committee exposure at OIA over a 13-year tenure demonstrates governance capability. The main question is whether his current treasury-heavy role at UFLEX has dulled his full-cycle finance skills — but his motivation to return to EPC is clear and genuine.
discussion_points: Progress to next stage — explore FP&A and statutory reporting currency

our_take_fragments: Deep EPC infrastructure finance — 15+ years in water sector construction before moving to UFLEX
JV creation experience directly relevant — built multiple joint ventures for infrastructure project awards
Consortium banking depth unmatched — manages 22 banks with €445M credit exposure
Treasury-heavy at UFLEX since leaving EPC — question is whether full-cycle FP&A skills remain current
Motivated to return to EPC — left construction 'by mistake' and wants back

candidates.flash_summary: Prior infrastructure and JV structuring experience (Freyssinet post-tensioning, Shivalaya highway projects €133M). Now full legal-entity ownership at Havas with APAC/EU reporting — strong end-to-end profile at smaller scale.
key_strengths: Freyssinet post-tensioning and geotechnics finance experience — closest industry match to Dywidag's specific business
Full legal-entity ownership of two Havas India entities with APAC and European reporting
JV structuring with 18% revenue savings through tax optimisation on Nagpur project

scope_match.candidate_actual: 9 finance staff across Delhi, Bangalore, Mumbai
Currently media; prior post-tensioning, HAM highways

key_criteria.name: End-to-End Finance Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Full legal entity ownership for two Havas India entities — budgeting, forecasting, statutory reporting, SOX compliance, ERP transformation, working capital, treasury. Leading ERP migration with March 2026 go-live.

key_criteria.name: Construction / EPC Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Prior infrastructure experience directly relevant to Dywidag — led IFRS 15 percentage-of-completion accounting at Freyssinet Menard (post-tensioning, geotechnics). Managed four HAM highway projects totalling €133M at Shivalaya.

key_criteria.name: JV Stakeholder Management
key_criteria.evidence: Structured JVs and AOP entities — created JV with supplier for Nagpur project, saving 18% revenue through tax optimisation and improving working capital where material costs were 70% of project value.

key_criteria.name: Board Governance & Legal Entity Responsibility
key_criteria.evidence: Board pack preparation and compliance oversight — manages global APAC audits, SOX compliance, and ISO certifications. Handles new project costing negotiations and client margin analysis.

key_criteria.name: International / Multinational Exposure
key_criteria.evidence: Multinational experience across French and German HQs — Havas (France), ThyssenKrupp (Germany), Freyssinet (France). APAC and European reporting lines.

compensation.flexibility: Acknowledges being underpaid. Appraisal due April 2026. Prioritising opportunity quality over immediate compensation.

why_interested.headline: Return to infrastructure-focused finance
why_interested.detail: Interested in moving back to an infrastructure platform where her Freyssinet post-tensioning and Shivalaya highway experience can be fully leveraged.

why_interested.headline: Global engineering company with 160-year legacy
why_interested.detail: Dywidag's 'local presence, global competence' model resonates directly with her background driving consolidation and controls across complex organisations.

why_interested.headline: Current role is smaller scale than ambition
why_interested.detail: Managing €5M in media feels like a step back from the €133M infrastructure portfolio she previously oversaw.

potential_concerns.concern: Current business size (€5M) is significantly smaller than the €20M JV — though prior Shivalaya portfolio was €133M.
Currently in media sector, not construction — her infrastructure experience is 2+ years old.

our_take.text: Megha has the most directly relevant technical background for Dywidag's specific business — her Freyssinet post-tensioning experience is an almost exact industry match. The JV structuring, IFRS 15 POC accounting, and multinational reporting experience are all directly applicable. Her current Havas role demonstrates full legal-entity ownership and governance discipline. The scale gap is real (€5M current vs €20M target) but her prior €133M infrastructure portfolio proves she can operate at larger scale. She's also significantly underpaid at ₹30L — the compensation conversation should be straightforward.
discussion_points: Progress to next stage — explore infrastructure network currency and readiness to return to construction pace

our_take_fragments: Most directly relevant technical background — Freyssinet post-tensioning experience is almost exact Dywidag industry match
IFRS 15 percentage-of-completion accounting, JV structuring, and MNC reporting all directly applicable
Scale gap is real (€5M current vs €20M target) — but prior €133M highway portfolio proves larger scale capability
Currently in media sector — infrastructure experience is 2+ years old, needs to ramp back up
Significantly underpaid at ₹30L — compensation conversation should be straightforward

candidates.flash_summary: Full-cycle startup CFO with board exposure, JV experience (Future Group/Reliance acquisition), and UK/Singapore operations. Currently driving 20x manufacturing capacity growth — but only in construction since 2024.
key_strengths: Full-cycle startup CFO with board director positions across Future Group subsidiaries
JV and M&A experience including due diligence for Future Group–Reliance acquisition
International operations management across London and Singapore offices

scope_match.candidate_actual: Managed UK (Cover Story) and Singapore (HT Media)

key_criteria.name: End-to-End Finance Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Full-cycle CFO with 10 direct reports — statutory reporting, FP&A, treasury, site accounting, project P&Ls. Secured €2.2M in startup funding from multiple banks despite company's three-year track record.

key_criteria.name: Construction / EPC Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Only in construction since 2024 — managing project cost tracking, contract clause analysis, and raw material price volatility for PEB manufacturing clients (ACC, DLF). Prior career entirely in media, retail, and telecom.

key_criteria.name: JV Stakeholder Management
key_criteria.evidence: Three JV experiences including major corporate transaction — served as Whole-Time Director during Future Group's acquisition by Reliance Industries, leading due diligence process. Also managed Wall Street Journal partnership at HT Media.

key_criteria.name: Board Governance & Legal Entity Responsibility
key_criteria.evidence: Board director positions across Future Group subsidiaries — monthly presentations to promoters alongside CEO at HT Media. Quarterly board meetings at current company covering growth plans and funding.

key_criteria.name: International / Multinational Exposure
key_criteria.evidence: Managed London design office (Cover Story) and Singapore publication office (HT Media) — controlled offshore operations and international reporting in both roles.

compensation.flexibility: Took a pay cut from ₹73.6L at Cover Story due to COVID-era job market. Has 5% equity in a WSB sister concern. Previous package was significantly higher.

why_interested.headline: Larger structured multinational platform
why_interested.detail: Interested in scaling beyond a startup into a more structured organisation with genuine corporate governance and international reporting.

why_interested.headline: Startup compensation below prior level
why_interested.detail: Took significant pay cut joining WSB after Cover Story was acquired by Reliance during COVID — this role would restore career-appropriate compensation.

potential_concerns.concern: Only in construction since 2024 — prior career spans media, retail, telecom, and fashion. Industry-specific finance depth is very recent.
Current company scale (€6.7M, 135 people) is significantly below the €20M / 500 employee target.
Compensation structure includes 50% cash payment — industry practice but introduces verification complexity.

our_take.text: Sarvesh brings genuine CFO-level ownership and surprisingly strong board governance experience, but from a much smaller and less complex platform. His Future Group due diligence experience and HT Media board presentations demonstrate real governance capability. The international operations management (London, Singapore) adds depth. However, his construction experience is less than two years old — his entire prior career was in unrelated sectors. The question is whether his adaptability and full-cycle CFO skills compensate for the industry knowledge gap.
discussion_points: Proceed with caution — assess construction finance depth and readiness for EPC-specific challenges

our_take_fragments: Genuine CFO-level ownership with 10 direct reports — full statutory, FP&A, treasury, and site accounting
Strong board governance — director positions at Future Group and monthly presentations at HT Media
Future Group–Reliance acquisition due diligence demonstrates M&A and JV capability
Construction experience only since 2024 — entire prior career in media, retail, and telecom
Scale gap significant — €6.7M company with 135 people vs €20M target with 500
